findings.title: SQL Injection via String Concatenation
findings.description: User-controlled input is concatenated directly into a SQL query without parameterization. An attacker can inject arbitrary SQL statements to read, modify, or delete database contents.
findings.code_snippet: String query = "SELECT * FROM users WHERE email = '" + email + "'";

remediation.description: Use parameterized queries instead of string concatenation
remediation.original_code:
String query = "SELECT * FROM users WHERE email = '" + email + "'";
ResultSet rs = stmt.executeQuery(query);

findings.title: Reflected Cross-Site Scripting (XSS)
findings.description: User input from the request parameter is written directly into the HTTP response without output encoding. An attacker can inject JavaScript that executes in other users' browsers.

remediation.description: HTML-encode user input before writing to response

findings.title: Hardcoded API Key
findings.description: An API key is hardcoded in the source file. If this code is committed to version control, the key is exposed to anyone with repository access.

remediation.description: Load the API key from an environment variable or secrets manager
